response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned full-stack engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background and genuine interest in developer tooling, but the role requires deep Java and Kotlin expertise, hands-on Spring ecosystem experience, and IDE internals knowledge — none of which are evident in this resume. Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms and a C IDE at NI where he wrote components, not contributed to JVM tooling), Kotlin is absent entirely, and there is no mention of Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate. His tooling work (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) is D-language-specific and does not translate to JVM language tooling. The skills mismatch on the core requirements is too significant for a mid-senior JVM tooling role.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and test infrastructure (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, CI/CD setup across multiple roles)
Experience shipping and maintaining open-source projects with real-world users (GISCollective, Green Map)
Broad systems experience including low-latency services, microservices, and embedded systems that shows engineering depth
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only superficial Java exposure; primary stack throughout career is TypeScript/Node.js and D language
Zero mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-based enterprise framework
No IDE internals experience — NI LabWindows role was writing C components for a C IDE, not contributing to JVM language tooling or IDE subsystems
